title: Preparation of Phosphate-buffered Saline (PBS)
author: Katy M Monteith
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.p4rdqv6
materials: Dissolve reagents in 800 mL of triple distilled H₂O using a magnetic stirrer and flea or by manual shaking.

steps.title: For a final concentration of 1x...
steps.instruction: For a final concentration of 1x PBS weigh out the following amount of each reagent: NaCl- 8g, KCl- 0.2 g, Na₂HPO4- 1.44 g, KH₂PO4- 0.24 g. For a final concentration of 10x PBS weigh out the following amount of each reagent: NaCl- 80g, KCl- 2 g, Na₂HPO4- 14.4 g, KH₂PO4- 2.4 g.

steps.title: Using a pH meter, adjust pH of solution to 7
steps.instruction: Using a pH meter, adjust pH of solution to 7.4 (or 7.2 if required) by adding concentrated hydrochloric acid (HCl).

steps.title: Add triple distilled H₂O to bring...
steps.instruction: Add triple distilled H₂O to bring final volume of solution to 1 L

steps.title: If require RNase free PBS, add...
steps.instruction: If require RNase free PBS, add 1 mL of DEPC (diethyl pyrocarbonate) per 1 L of PBS.
